custom.condition: The legal question is vague, compound, or materially underspecified.
custom.reason: The research scope must be confirmed before authorities are gathered or conclusions are drafted.

custom.condition: A legal proposition would be stated without user-provided or independently verified authority.
custom.reason: Unverified legal authority must remain a visible placeholder and may not be presented as existing or current law.

custom.condition: The requested work product is a formal legal opinion letter or an immediate client-facing legal conclusion.
custom.reason: Formal opinions and real-time legal advice require direct attorney authorship and professional-responsibility review.

execution_modes.purpose: Refine the question presented, identify jurisdiction and factual gaps, propose a research plan, and list authority types to verify without drafting a substantive conclusion.
Produce the complete attorney-review research memo with verified authority, explicit assumptions, qualified analysis, and open verification items.
Run the standard memo plus adverse-authority, currentness, negative-treatment, proposition-support, and citation-integrity passes for each material proposition.

input_schema.label: Specific legal question presented
input_schema.description: A discrete legal question with confirmed scope.

input_schema.label: Known facts
input_schema.description: The legally material facts, each attributable to the user or a provided source.

input_schema.label: Applicable jurisdiction
input_schema.description: The jurisdiction and body of law governing the question.

input_schema.label: Authority already provided
input_schema.description: Cases, statutes, regulations, rules, or secondary sources already supplied for verification and use.

output_schema.label: Structured legal research memo using IRAC discipline for attorney review
output_schema.description: Question presented, brief answer, facts, assumptions, issue-by-issue analysis, conclusion, authorities, and open attorney-verification items.

output_schema.label: Claim-to-authority map
output_schema.description: Every material legal proposition mapped to the exact authority and locator that supports it, including verification status and contrary authority.

fields.description: Whether the authority is user-provided, connector-verified, independently verified, secondary-only, or unresolved.
The precise proposition for which the source is cited.
The recorded treatment status when a negative-treatment check is performed.

modules.load_when: the memo uses multiple authority types or citation forms
US federal case existence or citation verification is available through CourtListener
deep-review mode or when a material case controls the analysis
